subject: GRB 200924B: Fermi GBM Final Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 05:38:29 UT on 24 Sep 2020, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 200924B (trigger 622618714 / 200924235).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 319.4, Dec = 6.5 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 21h 18m, +6d 28'), with a statistical uncertainty of 3.8 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 71.2 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200924235/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn200924235.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200924235/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn200924235.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200924235/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn200924235.gif
